title: Logic Apps Management REST API — Runs, Triggers, Enable/Disable, Resubmit

confirmed_facts.fact: Auth: ARM scope (https://management.azure.com/.default). Requires Logic App Contributor or Contributor role on the resource group.
Get workflow definition: GET .../workflows/{name}?api-version=2019-05-01 — returns full resource including properties.definition (the workflow JSON), properties.state, and properties.parameters
List workflow runs: GET .../workflows/{name}/runs?api-version=2019-05-01&$top=25&$filter=status eq 'Failed' — supports OData filter on status, startTime
Run status values: Running, Waiting, Succeeded, Failed, Cancelled, Skipped, Suspended, TimedOut, Aborted
Get run actions: GET .../runs/{runId}/actions?api-version=2019-05-01 — returns each action with status, startTime, endTime, inputs, outputs, error
Get action input/output: GET .../runs/{runId}/actions/{actionName}/contentVersion/{version}/inputs or /outputs — returns the actual payload (useful for debugging)
List trigger history: GET .../triggers/{triggerName}/histories?api-version=2019-05-01 — shows fired, not fired (no new data), and failed evaluations
Run trigger manually: POST .../triggers/{triggerName}/run?api-version=2019-05-01 — executes the workflow immediately (useful for testing). For Request triggers, POST the callback URL instead.
Get callback URL for Request trigger: POST .../triggers/{triggerName}/listCallbackUrl?api-version=2019-05-01 — returns {value: 'https://...', basePath, queries: {sig, sv, sp}}
Enable/disable workflow: PATCH .../workflows/{name}?api-version=2019-05-01 with body {properties: {state: 'Enabled'}} or {properties: {state: 'Disabled'}}
Resubmit a failed run: POST .../runs/{runId}/resubmit?api-version=2019-05-01 — creates a new run with the same trigger inputs. Only works on completed (Failed/Succeeded) runs.
List all Logic Apps in a resource group: GET /subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rgName}/providers/Microsoft.Logic/workflows?api-version=2019-05-01

Deploy/update workflow definition: PUT .../workflows/{name}?api-version=2019-05-01 with full resource body (location, identity, properties.definition, properties.parameters). This is an idempotent create-or-update.
Filter runs by time: $filter=startTime ge 2026-04-01T00:00:00Z and startTime lt 2026-04-11T00:00:00Z — ISO 8601 format, no quotes around the datetime

implementation_notes:
List failed runs in the last 7 days:
```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

def list_failed_runs(session, base_url, days=7):
    since = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=days)).strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')
    url = f'{base_url}/runs'
    params = {
        'api-version': '2019-05-01',
        '$filter': f"status eq 'Failed' and startTime ge {since}",
        '$top': 50,
    }
    results = []
    while url:
        resp = session.get(url, params=params, timeout=(10, 30))
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        results.extend(data.get('value', []))
        url = data.get('nextLink')
        params = None
    return results
```

Resubmit all failed runs:
```python
def resubmit_failed(session, base_url, run_ids):
    results = []
    for run_id in run_ids:
        url = f'{base_url}/runs/{run_id}/resubmit?api-version=2019-05-01'
        resp = session.post(url, timeout=(10, 30))
        results.append({'run_id': run_id, 'status': resp.status_code})
    return results
```

Enable/disable a workflow:
```python
def set_workflow_state(session, base_url, enabled=True):
    state = 'Enabled' if enabled else 'Disabled'
    url = f'{base_url}?api-version=2019-05-01'
    # GET current definition first (PUT requires full body)
    current = session.get(url, timeout=(10, 30)).json()
    current['properties']['state'] = state
    resp = session.put(url, json=current, timeout=(10, 30))
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()
```
caveats: api-version 2019-05-01 is the stable version for Consumption Logic Apps. Standard (single-tenant) uses a different API surface.
Resubmit creates a NEW run — it does not retry the existing run. The original failed run stays in Failed state.
PATCH for state change is simpler but some environments require PUT with the full body — if PATCH returns 405, fall back to GET+PUT.
The /runs endpoint returns max 250 results per page. For large volumes, use $filter with time ranges.
Trigger callback URLs contain a SAS signature that expires — regenerate periodically for long-lived integrations.
